title: 10 best herbal essential oils for lower back pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for lower back pain are natural remedies derived from plants that provide relief from discomfort and inflammation in the lower back region. They work by reducing muscle tension, improving circulation, and fighting inflammation. The best herbal essential oils for lower back pain include Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Curcuma longa, Cinnamomum verum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Boswellia serrata, Cymbopogon citratus, Copaifera officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Melaleuca alternifolia. If left untreated, lower back pain can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, mood swings, and reduced productivity, ultimately affecting relationships and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce muscle tension and relieve strain on the spine. The citrusy scent also has a calming effect, easing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass oil's natural analgesic properties help to dull persistent aches and pains, promoting relaxation and rejuvenation of the entire body.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemongrass essential oil helps to relax muscles and relieve spasms, which can provide relief from lower back pain caused by muscle tension or inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass essential oil can help reduce swelling and pain associated with lower back pain conditions such as sciatica or herniated discs.

Analgesic property: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to have analgesic properties, which means it can help numb the area affected by lower back pain, providing temporary relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle and joint pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, relieving back pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning, collecting at least one pound for processing.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight, for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves using a distillation apparatus with water and steam, collecting the essential oil for 4-6 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment, resulting in a clear yellowish liquid.
Store the obtained cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, kept away from light and heat, for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it can help reduce muscle tension and ease stiffness, providing relief from chronic backaches. The oil's natural antioxidant compounds also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common triggers for lower back pain. Additionally, tea tree essential oils' warming sensation can increase blood flow and circulation, further alleviating discomfort and promoting healing in the lower back region.
remedy_properties: Wintergreen Essential Oil (Gaultheria procumbens): Known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints.
Peppermint Essential Oil (Mentha piperita): Has a cooling effect that can help relax tense muscles and improve circulation, which may help alleviate lower back pain.
Frankincense Essential Oil (Boswellia serrata): Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce pain and swelling in the lower back by reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, tea tree leaves are a popular choice for making essential oils that can help soothe and relieve lower back pain.

Buds: The buds of the tea tree plant contain high concentrations of compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and dry them in a warm place for three hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a copper still with a ratio of one part leaves to 3 parts water for two hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper coffee filter into a clean glass vial.
Combine the filtered essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 1:10 dilution ratio and mix well.
Store the finished blend in an amber glass bottle and use within six months, applying topically to affected areas three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, work to reduce muscle tension and joint stiffness, alleviating discomfort and pain in the lower back region. Additionally, its warming and soothing properties help to relax the muscles and calm any inflammation, allowing for improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_properties: Inflammation Reduction: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil contains compounds like eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints of the lower back.

Pain Relief: The essential oil's active ingredients, including cineole and α-pinene, have been shown to have a calming effect on nerve endings, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with lower back issues.

Muscle Relaxation: Eucalyptus globulus oil contains compounds that can help relax muscle spasms, reduce tension, and ease stiffness in the muscles of the lower back, making it an effective natural remedy for managing chronic back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the lower back.
Barks: The bark of the plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves in early morning dew, avoiding direct sunlight exposure.
Clean and sort the harvested leaves removing any debris or damaged leaves, ensuring proper sanitation.
Steam distill 500g of eucalyptus globulus leaves with 1 liter of pure water at 100°c for 2 hours, collecting the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment, preserving its potency.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing it in a cool place to maintain quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with lower back pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints. The oil's natural relaxation response can also reduce muscle tension, which is often a contributing factor to lower back pain. Additionally, lavender's calming scent can promote deep breathing and relaxation, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being. This natural remedy can be used in a topical application or through aromatherapy to provide effective relief from lower back pain.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory: English Lavender oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and swelling in the lower back, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness.

Muscle Relaxant: The oil's muscle-relaxing properties help to ease tense muscles in the back, hips, and glutes, which can contribute to lower back pain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.

Antispasmodic: English Lavender oil's antispasmodic properties help to calm and soothe cramping and spasticity in the muscles, including those in the lower back, reducing the risk of further strain or injury.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate lower back pain.
Buds: Contain high amounts of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-spasmodic effects, making them effective in reducing muscle tension and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and leaves in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material with distilled water at a ratio of 10:1 for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter or paper towel within 24 hours.
Store the final lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting cap at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Balsam fir essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints. The oil's analgesic qualities also help to reduce discomfort and numb the sensation of pain, providing quick relief from chronic or acute back pain. Additionally, the refreshing and invigorating aroma of balsam fir essential oils can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common contributors to lower back pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Balsam Fir essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the muscles and joints associated with lower back pain.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic compounds present in Balsam Fir essential oil can help numb or dull the sensation of pain, providing relief from lower back discomfort and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant: The camphene and bornyl acetate components of Balsam Fir essential oil have a calming effect on muscles, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension that contribute to lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for relieving lower back pain.
Bark: Containing high levels of terpinen-4-ol, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms, making it beneficial for lower back pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature copaifera officinalis leaves and twigs with scissors for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Crush the dried material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle for efficient steam distillation.
Steam distill the crushed copaifera officinalis material with 1:3 solvent-to-material ratio at 100°c for 2 hours.
Filter and separate the essential oil from the distilled water and solvents using a coffee filter paper within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with lower back pain because it contains compounds that have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and relaxant properties. These properties help to reduce muscle spasms, ease tension, and promote relaxation in the muscles and joints of the lower back. Additionally, the warming sensation of the oil can help to increase blood flow and reduce stiffness, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort associated with lower back strain and arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curry leaf contains compounds like cinnamic acid and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and ease muscle strain in the lower back.
Pain relief: The volatile oils present in curry leaf, such as eugenol and geraniol, can help to numb the pain receptors, providing natural analgesia for relief from lower back discomfort.
Antioxidant: Curry leaf is rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to chronic lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the roots are a good source of anti-inflammatory compounds that help relieve lower back pain by reducing swelling and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome's unique combination of bioactive compounds helps to relax muscles and reduce stiffness in the lower back, providing relief from chronic pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh helichrysum italicum leaves and stems in the early morning dew for maximum potency.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Dry the washed plant material in a well-ventilated area out of direct sunlight at room temperature, allowing 7-10 days for complete evaporation.
Steam distill the dried helichrysum italicum plant material using a small-scale still with 1 liter of distilled water and monitoring the distillation process for approximately 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, storing it in a cool, dark place to preserve its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease muscle tension in the affected area. The oil's analgesic properties also provide relief from pain and discomfort, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, geranium oil has been known to relax and soothe the muscles, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calm, which can contribute to a reduction in back pain symptoms.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Wild Geranium essential oil has analgesic properties, which means it can help reduce or eliminate pain associated with lower back strain and tension.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints of the lower back, which can contribute to chronic pain relief.
Muscle relaxant: Wild Geranium essential oil has a calming effect on muscle spasms and cramps, making it effective in relieving tension and discomfort in the lower back caused by strain or overuse.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild geranium plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving lower back pain.
Barks: The barks of the wild geranium plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh geranium maculatum leaves at dawn on a dry day in the summer.
Steam-distill 500 grams of harvested leaves using 1000 milliliters of distilled water for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Mix the stored essential oil with 10 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint oil can help reduce muscle spasms and tension in the lower back, providing relief from chronic discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help decrease inflammation and stiffness, promoting relaxation and reducing the feeling of heat or pain. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals suffering from sciatica, herniated discs, or other lower back conditions.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint essential oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back, providing relief from conditions such as sciatica and herniated discs.

Muscle Relaxant Properties: The muscle relaxant properties of peppermint essential oil help to ease tension and spasms in the muscles of the lower back, which can contribute to conditions like chronic back pain.

Circulatory Stimulant Properties: Peppermint essential oil's circulatory stimulant properties help to improve blood flow and reduce stagnation in the lower back area, which can help to alleviate conditions such as stiffness and numbness in the legs and feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving muscle and joint pain associated with lower back pain.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant are rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and ease pain in the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase dried peppermint herb from a reputable supplier.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 100 degrees fahrenheit for two hours or air dry them overnight.
Combine 20 grams of dried peppermint herb with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar and let it steep for 48 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids then add 100 milliliters of water and stir well.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for at least two weeks before decanting and using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compound, boswellic acid, works to reduce swelling and relieve discomfort in the muscles and joints. When applied topically or inhaled, frankincense oil can help relax tense muscles and alleviate stiffness, providing fast relief from lower back pain. Additionally, its natural decongestant properties may also help to reduce tension and improve overall circulation, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the joints and muscles, alleviating pain associated with lower back strain.

Pain-relieving: The boswellic acids present in frankincense essential oil have been shown to have analgesic and anti-pain properties, helping to numb the sensation of pain and provide relief from chronic back pain.

Antioxidant: Frankincense essential oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which is thought to contribute to lower back pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with lower back strain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate lower back pain.
Buds: Frankincense buds are particularly effective in relieving muscle tension and stiffness in the lower back, thanks to their high levels of beta-boswellic acid.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata leaves and dry them in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least three days.
Combine one pound of dried boswellia serrata leaves with two cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture in a slow cooker on low for eight hours to extract the essential oils from the herbs.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.
Allow the remaining liquid to cool and decant it into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. The oil's warming and soothing properties also help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, allowing for a reduction in stiffness and pain. Additionally, sage essential oil contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving lower back pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Sage essential oil helps to relax muscle spasms and cramps associated with lower back pain, reducing discomfort and stiffness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to lower back pain relief.
Pain-relieving properties: Sage essential oil contains analgesic and anesthetic compounds that help to numb or desensitize painful areas of the body, providing temporary relief from lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots are used to create essential oils that soothe and reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to relax muscles and ease tension in the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh sclarea leaves ensuring at least 20 pounds for optimal yields.
Clean and dry the sclarea leaves thoroughly eliminating any debris or contaminants.
Steam distill the dried sclarea leaves using a clevenger apparatus at 100-150°f for 30 minutes.
Monitor the distillation process until a clear essential oil forms collecting it in a dark glass vial.
Filter the essential oil through paper towel or coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat lower back pain with herbal essential oils is to combine them with massage therapy. Essential oils such as lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and relax the muscles. Apply a few drops of oil to the affected area, then gently massage it in using gentle, circular motions. This can provide quick relief from pain and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lower back pain that are treated with herbal essential oils are sciatica, arthritis, fibromyalgia, and muscle strains. Essential oils like Wintergreen, Birch, and Ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort in these conditions. Other oils such as Lavender and Peppermint can also provide relief by reducing tension and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate associated symptoms like stiffness and fatigue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and warming action. The oil contains compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to reduce muscle spasms, relax tense muscles, and decrease pain perception. Regular use of ginger essential oil can also improve circulation, reduce swelling, and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating lower back pain and promoting overall well-being. It's often used in aromatherapy to provide relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with lower back pain.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the lower back region.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds are often used to create essential oils, containing volatile oils that help to ease pain and discomfort associated with lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning or late evening when the plant is at its peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested roots in a 500ml still for 3 hours at 100 degrees celsius to extract the oil.
Collect the distilled oil in a clean 100ml glass container and store it in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius.
Filter the oil using a 10 micron filter paper to remove any impurities and sediment that may have formed during storage.
Label and date the filtered oil and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months in a tightly sealed glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with lower back pain because it reduces inflammation and relaxes tense muscles. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties ease muscle spasms and joint pain, allowing the body to heal naturally. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to clear mucus from the lungs, which can contribute to lower back pain. The invigorating scent of Eucalyptus globulus also has a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, ultimately providing relief from lower back pain.
remedy_constituents: 1,8-Cineole: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
α-Pinene: As a natural bronchodilator and anti-inflammatory agent, α-Pinene can help relax muscles, reduce spasms, and alleviate pain in the lower back region.
Caryophyllene oxide: This terpene has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain, inflammation, and stiffness in the lower back, promoting relaxation and relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of eucalyptus oil, which contains compounds like eucalyptol that help to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle tension.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark contains a high concentration of eucalyptol, which is used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the lower back.
Stems: The stems of the Eucalyptus globulus plant also contain eucalyptol, making them another useful source of essential oil for relieving lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants in the early morning to capture oils.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 2 pounds of the dried leaves for 2-3 hours to separate essential oils.
Collect and filter the distilled essential oils to remove impurities and excess water.
Store the herbal eucalyptus globulus essential oils in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. It blocks the production of pain-causing enzymes and reduces inflammation in the muscles and joints, providing relief from lower back pain. Additionally, the oil's warming and relaxing properties can help ease muscle spasms and promote a sense of calm, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating lower back pain and improving overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids (including Curcumin): These compounds exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with lower back pain.
Beta-Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the lower back region.
Eugenol: This phenolic compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for essential oil production, as they contain the highest concentration of curcuminoids, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate lower back pain.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa also contain curcuminoids, although in smaller amounts than the rhyzomes, making them a secondary choice for essential oil production.
Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce lower back pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh turmeric rhizomes when they are firm and the skin is smooth.
Dry the rhizomes in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes in a distillation apparatus at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the turmeric essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter.
Store the turmeric essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which have been found to reduce muscle spasms and relax tense muscles. It also has a warming effect, increasing blood flow to the affected area and promoting healing. As a result, Cinnamomum verum essential oil can provide relief from chronic lower back pain, stiffness, and discomfort, allowing individuals to move more freely and comfortably. Regular use can lead to improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Linalool: As an analgesic agent, linalool can help block pain signals in the body, providing relief from lower back pain.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress contributing to lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils that contain eugenol, a compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve lower back pain.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make essential oils that contain cinnamaldehyde and linalool, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature bark in the morning after the dew has dried completely and the temperature is mild.
Steam distill the harvested bark in a large distillation unit at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation unit using a condenser and a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and particles.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease muscle tension. The oil's analgesic properties also help to alleviate pain and discomfort, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to repair and regenerate damaged tissues, improving overall lower back health. By using Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils, individuals can experience relief from lower back pain and improve their overall well-being and quality of life. Regular use can lead to long-term pain management.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back region.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with lower back conditions.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of essential oil for Rosmarinus officinalis, as they contain the high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to relieve lower back pain.
Flowers: Rosmarinus officinalis flowers are also used to produce essential oil, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation associated with lower back pain.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis contain a smaller but still significant amount of essential oil, which is often used in combination with leaves and flowers to create a balanced blend for lower back pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature rosmarinus officinalis plants in the morning when they are at peak potency level.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill 20 kg of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves using 100 kg of water for 4 to 6 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or a still and collect 200 ml of oil.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed tightly with a dropper cap for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the affected area. It also helps to relax muscles and improve blood flow, which can contribute to a reduction in lower back pain. Additionally, its natural anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce stiffness and improve mobility in individuals suffering from lower back pain. This makes it a popular natural remedy for alleviating lower back pain.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This constituent acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent that helps reduce swelling and pain in the lower back by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Boswellic acids: These compounds have shown to inhibit the activity of 5-lipoxygenase, an enzyme responsible for producing leukotrienes, which are pro-inflammatory mediators contributing to lower back pain.

Terpinen-4-ol: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and inflammation in the lower back by interacting with pain receptors and reducing the production of pain-causing chemicals.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the primary source of frankincense oil, which is commonly used to reduce inflammation and alleviate lower back pain.
Barks: The resin from the bark is often used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to ease lower back pain.

Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids, which are used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in managing lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh boswellia serrata resin, typically obtained by making incisions in the tree trunk.
Steam distill the harvested resin in a stainless steel distillation apparatus at 100-150mmhg pressure.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities or particulate matter.
Store the final boswellia serrata essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with lower back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil, commonly known as lemongrass oil, contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have analgesic and antispasmodic effects. These properties help to reduce muscle tension and inflammation in the lower back, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties can also help to improve circulation and reduce swelling, further alleviating lower back pain. Regular use can promote relaxation and reduce pain.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Geraniol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, contributing to the relief of muscle tension and pain in the lower back region.
Caryophyllene: Shows potential in reducing inflammation and promoting pain relief by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system, providing relief from lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cymbopogon citratus are primarily used to make essential oils for lower back pain due to their high content of limonene, a compound known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used to make essential oils for lower back pain as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to relieve muscle tension.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are another part used to make essential oils for lower back pain due to their high concentration of essential oils, which include compounds like citral and geraniol that have pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 kg of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and tie them in small bunches to dry.
Steam distill the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves at 100°c for 2 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a filter paper to remove impurities slowly.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dark place to preserve the oil's quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Copaifera officinalis essential oils helps with lower back pain because it contains a unique blend of terpenes, including caryophyllene, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This natural oil has been traditionally used in folk medicine to reduce swelling and ease muscle tension, making it an effective treatment for lower back pain. Copaifera officinalis essential oils also has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, allowing it to provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with lower back pain. Its natural properties make it a popular choice for pain management.
remedy_constituents: Borneol: It helps with lower back pain by reducing inflammation and providing analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the affected area.
α-Pinene: This terpene helps with lower back pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, which can help improve mobility and reduce stiffness in the lower back.
β-Pinene: It helps with lower back pain by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation, which can help alleviate pain and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Barks: Copaifera officinalis barks are used to make essential oils due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Copaifera officinalis leaves are used to make essential oils as they contain terpenoids that have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate lower back pain.

Buds: Copaifera officinalis buds are used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and pain in the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Collect copaifera officinalis resin in the early morning to ensure its highest quality and potency.
Steam distill the collected resin using a distillation apparatus for 3-4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel after distillation is complete.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a cotton cloth to remove any impurities and debris present.
Store the herbal copaifera officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with lower back pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties. The oil's calming scent can reduce muscle tension and spasms in the lower back region, providing relief from pain and discomfort. It also has analgesic and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate chronic lower back pain and improve sleep quality, contributing to a faster recovery and reduced pain levels. Its soothing aroma promotes overall relaxation and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with lower back pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, thereby alleviating muscle tension and discomfort.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene derivative, it possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to ease lower back pain and promote a sense of calm.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils to help alleviate lower back pain due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils to help reduce lower back pain due to their ability to relax muscles and ease tension.

Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils to help ease lower back pain due to their analgesic and antispasmodic properties that reduce inflammation and muscle spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a 2:1 ratio of water to flowers for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities and particles.
Bottle the pure lavandula angustifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles and label it properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with lower back pain because it contains compounds like caryophyllene and alpha-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the lower back, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. The oil's antimicrobial properties can also help prevent infections that may exacerbate lower back pain, while its natural decongestant properties can help improve circulation and reduce tension in the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps reduce inflammation and ease pain in the lower back by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort in the lower back region.
Limonene: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps reduce muscle spasms and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of Tea Tree oil, which is commonly used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain essential oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate lower back pain.
Barks: The bark of the Tea Tree tree is another source of essential oils that have been traditionally used to treat muscle and joint pain, including lower back pain.
